paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: To biomedical researchers all over the world, twins offer a precious opportunity to untangle the influence of genes and the environment - of nature and nurture. Because identical twins come from a single fertilized egg that splits into two, they share virtually the same genetic code. Any differences between them - one twin having younger looking skin, for example - must be due to environmental factors such as less time spent in the sun.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Alternatively, by comparing the experiences of identical twins with those of fraternal twins, who come from separate eggs and share on average half their DNA, researchers can quantify the extent to which our genes affect our lives. If identical twins are more similar to each other with respect to an ailment than fraternal twins are, then vulnerability to the disease must be rooted at least in part in heredity.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: These two lines of research - studying the differences between identical twins to pinpoint the influence of environment, and comparing identical twins with fraternal ones to measure the role of inheritance - have been crucial to understanding the interplay of nature and nurture in determining our personalities, behavior, and vulnerability to disease.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The idea of using twins to measure the influence of heredity dates back to 1875, when the English scientist Francis Galton first suggested the approach (and coined the phrase 'nature and nurture'). But twin studies took a surprising twist in the 1980s, with the arrival of studies into identical twins who had been separated at birth and reunited as adults. Over two decades 137 sets of twins eventually visited Thomas Bouchard's lab in what became known as the Minnesota Study of Twins Reared Apart. Numerous tests were carried out on the twins, and they were each asked more than 15,000 questions.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Bouchard and his colleagues used this mountain of data to identify how far twins were affected by their genetic makeup. The key to their approach was a statistical concept called heritability. In broad terms, the heritability of a trait measures the extent to which differences among members of a population can be explained by differences in their genetics. And wherever Bouchard and other scientists looked, it seemed, they found the invisible hand of genetic influence helping to shape our lives.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Lately, however, twin studies have helped lead scientists to a radical new conclusion: that nature and nurture are not the only elemental forces at work. According to a recent field called epigenetics, there is a third factor also in play, one that in some cases serves as a bridge between the environment and our genes, and in others operates on its own to shape who we are.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Epigenetic processes are chemical reactions tied to neither nature nor nurture but representing what researchers have called a 'third component'. These reactions influence how our genetic code is expressed: how each gene is strengthened or weakened, even turned on or off, to build our bones, brains and all the other parts of our bodies.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: If you think of our DNA as an immense piano keyboard and our genes as the keys - each key symbolizing a segment of DNA responsible for a particular note, or trait, and all the keys combining to make us who we are - then epigenetic processes determine when and how each key can be struck, changing the tune being played.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: One way the study of epigenetics is revolutionizing our understanding of biology is by revealing a mechanism by which the environment directly impacts on genes. Studies of animals, for example, have shown that when a rat experiences stress during pregnancy, it can cause epigenetic changes in a fetus that lead to behavioral problems as the rodent grows up. Other epigenetic processes appear to occur randomly, while others are normal, such as those that guide embryonic cells as they become heart, brain, or liver cells, for example.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Geneticist Danielle Reed has worked with many twins over the years and thought deeply about what twin studies have taught us. 'It's very clear when you look at twins that much of what they share is hardwired,' she says. 'Many things about them are absolutely the same and unalterable. But it's also clear, when you get to know them, that other things about them are different. Epigenetics is the origin of a lot of those differences, in my view.'

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Reed credits Thomas Bouchard's work for today's surge in twin studies. 'He was the trailblazer,' she says. 'We forget that 50 years ago things like heart disease were thought to be caused entirely by lifestyle. Schizophrenia was thought to be due to poor mothering. Twin studies have allowed us to be more reflective about what people are actually born with and what's caused by experience.'

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: Having said that, Reed adds, the latest work in epigenetics promises to take our understanding even further. 'What I like to say is that nature writes some things in pencil and some things in pen,' she says. 'Things written in pen you can't change. That's DNA. But things written in pencil you can. That's epigenetics. Now that we're actually able to look at the DNA and see where the pencil writings are, it's sort of a whole new world.'

questions.title: There may be genetic causes for the differences in how young the skin of identical twins looks.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "genetic causes" 和 "identical twins" 在原文中对应 "genetic code" 和 "identical twins"。原文明确指出，"Any differences between them... must be due to environmental factors"，即双胞胎之间的差异是由于环境因素，而不是基因原因。因此，题干中的信息与文章内容相反，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Any differences between them - one twin having younger looking skin, for example - must be due to environmental factors such as less time spent in the sun.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提到双胞胎比非双胞胎更容易患某些疾病的信息。文章主要讨论的是双胞胎研究如何帮助理解基因和环境对疾病的影响，而不是直接比较双胞胎和非双胞胎的患病风险。学生需要通过抓住关键词并在文章中寻找对应信息来解答此类题目。理解文章的主旨和细节信息是解答此类题目的关键。

central_sentences.sentence: Epigenetic processes are chemical reactions tied to neither nature nor nurture but representing what researchers have called a 'third component'. paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Though we might think of film as an essentially visual experience, we really cannot afford to underestimate the importance of film sound. A meaningful soundtrack is often as complicated as the image on the screen, and is ultimately just as much the responsibility of the director. The entire soundtrack consists of three essential ingredients: the human voice, sound effects and music. These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects. Topics which essentially refer to the three previously mentioned tracks are discussed below. They include dialogue, synchronous and asynchronous sound effects, and music.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Let us start with dialogue. As is the case with stage drama, dialogue serves to tell the story and expresses feelings and motivations of characters as well. Often with film characterization the audience perceives little or no difference between the character and the actor. Thus, for example, the actor Humphrey Bogart is the character Sam Spade; film personality and life personality seem to merge. Perhaps this is because the very texture of a performer's voice supplies an element of character.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: When voice textures fit the performer's physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges. The viewer sees not an actor working at his craft, but another human being struggling with life. It is interesting to note that how dialogue is used and the very amount of dialogue used varies widely among films. For example, in the highly successful science-fiction film 2001, little dialogue was evident, and most of it was banal and of little intrinsic interest. In this way the filmmaker was able to portray what Thomas Sobochack and Vivian Sobochack call, in An Introduction to Film, the 'inadequacy of human responses when compared with the magnificent technology created by man and the visual beauties of the universe'.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The comedy Bringing Up Baby, on the other hand, presents practically non-stop dialogue delivered at breakneck speed. This use of dialogue underscores not only the dizzy quality of the character played by Katherine Hepburn, but also the absurdity of the film itself and thus its humor. The audience is bounced from gag to gag and conversation to conversation; there is no time for audience reflection. The audience is caught up in a whirlwind of activity in simply managing to follow the plot. This film presents pure escapism – largely due to its frenetic dialogue.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Synchronous sound effects are those sounds which are synchronized or matched with what is viewed. For example, if the film portrays a character playing the piano, the sounds of the piano are projected. Synchronous sounds contribute to the realism of film and also help to create a particular atmosphere. For example, the 'click' of a door being opened may simply serve to convince the audience that the image portrayed is real, and the audience may only subconsciously note the expected sound. However, if the 'click' of an opening door is part of an ominous action such as a burglary, the sound mixer may call attention to the 'click' with an increase in volume; this helps to engage the audience in a moment of suspense.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Asynchronous sound effects, on the other hand, are not matched with a visible source of the sound on screen. Such sounds are included so as to provide an appropriate emotional nuance, and they may also add to the realism of the film. For example, a filmmaker might opt to include the background sound of an ambulance's siren while the foreground sound and image portrays an arguing couple. The asynchronous ambulance siren underscores the psychic injury incurred in the argument; at the same time the noise of the siren adds to the realism of the film by acknowledging the film's city setting.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence. We are aware that it is used to add emotion and rhythm. Usually not meant to be noticeable, it often provides a tone or an emotional attitude toward the story and/or the characters depicted. In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood. For example, dissonant music may be used in film to indicate an approaching (but not yet visible) menace or disaster.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes. For example, a particular musical theme associated with an individual character or situation may be repeated at various points in a film in order to remind the audience of salient motifs or ideas.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Film sound comprises conventions and innovations. We have come to expect an acceleration of music during car chases and creaky doors in horror films. Yet, it is important to note as well that sound is often brilliantly conceived. The effects of sound are often largely subtle and often are noted by only our subconscious minds. We need to foster an awareness of film sound as well as film space so as to truly appreciate an art form that sprang to life during the twentieth century – the modern film.

questions.title: In the first paragraph, the writer makes a point that

options.text: the director should plan the sound track at an early stage in filming.
it would be wrong to overlook the contribution of sound to the artistry of films.
the music industry can have a beneficial influence on sound in film.
it is important for those working on the sound in a film to have sole responsibility for it

questions.analysis: 文章第一段明确指出“我们真的不能低估电影声音的重要性”，这与选项B“忽视声音对电影艺术的贡献是错误的”表达了相同的意思。因此，正确答案是B。

central_sentences.sentence: Though we might think of film as an essentially visual experience, we really cannot afford to underestimate the importance of film sound.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然我们可能认为电影本质上是一种视觉体验，但我们实在不能低估电影声音的重要性。

questions.title: One reason that the writer refers to Humphrey Bogart is to exemplify

options.text: the importance of the actor and the character appearing to have similar personalities.
the audience's wish that actors are visually appropriate for their roles.
the value of the actor having had similar feelings to the character.
the audience's preference for dialogue to be as authentic as possible.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为文章明确指出了演员和角色个性相似的重要性。文章中提到观众认为角色和演员之间几乎没有区别，这与选项A中的“演员和角色看起来拥有相似的个性的重要性”直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Often with film characterization the audience perceives little or no difference between the character and the actor. Thus, for example, the actor Humphrey Bogart is the character Sam Spade; film personality and life personality seem to merge.
central_sentences.translation: 在电影角色塑造中，观众常常感觉角色和演员之间几乎没有差别。因此，例如，演员汉弗莱·鲍嘉就是角色山姆·斯派德；电影中的人格和现实生活中的人格似乎融为一体。

target_sen.target_sen: Thus, for example, the actor Humphrey Bogart is the character Sam Spade; film personality and life personality seem to merge.
Thus, for example, the actor Humphrey Bogart is the character Sam Spade; film personality and life personality seem to merge.

questions.title: In the third paragraph, the writer suggests that

options.text: audiences are likely to be critical of film dialogue that does not reflect their own experience.
film dialogue that appears to be dull may have a specific purpose.
filmmakers vary considerably in the skill with which they handle dialogue.
the most successful films are those with dialogue of a high quality.

questions.analysis: 文章提到在电影《2001》中，对话很少且乏味，但这种乏味的对话有其特定的目的，即表现人类反应的不足。因此，正确答案是B：film dialogue that appears to be dull may have a specific purpose。

central_sentences.sentence: in the highly successful science-fiction film 2001, little dialogue was evident, and most of it was banal and of little intrinsic interest. In this way the film-maker was able to portray what Thomas Sobochack and Vivian Sobochack call, in An Introduction to Film, the 'inadequacy of human responses when compared with the magnificent technology created by man and the visual beauties of the universe
central_sentences.translation: 在非常成功的科幻电影《2001》中，对话很少，而且大多平淡无奇，没什么内在意义。通过这种方式，电影制作者能够表现出托马斯·索博查克和维维安·索博查克在《电影入门》中所说的"与人类创造的宏伟技术和宇宙视觉美相比，人类反应的不足"。

questions.title: What does the writer suggest about Bringing Up Baby?

options.text: The plot suffers from the filmmaker's wish to focus on humorous dialogue.
The dialogue helps to make it one of the best comedy films ever produced.
There is a mismatch between the speed of the dialogue and the speed of actions.
The nature of the dialogue emphasises key elements of the film.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是D，因为文章明确指出《Bringing Up Baby》的对话不仅强调了角色的特质，还突出了影片的荒诞性和幽默感，这些都是影片的关键元素。选项D中的“the nature of the dialogue emphasises key elements of the film”与文章中的描述完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: This use of dialogue underscores not only the dizzy quality of the character played by Katherine Hepburn, but also the absurdity of the film itself and thus its humor.
central_sentences.translation: 这种对话的运用不仅强调了凯瑟琳·赫本扮演的角色的晕眩特质，还突出了电影本身的荒诞性，从而增加了幽默感。

questions.title: The writer refers to the `click' of a door to make the point that realistic sounds

options.text: are often used to give the audience a false impression of events in the film.
may be interpreted in different ways by different members of the audience.
may be modified in order to manipulate the audience's response to the film.
tend to be more significant in films presenting realistic situations.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章明确提到声音可以通过调整（如增加音量）来影响观众的反应（如制造悬念）。通过找到题干和文章中的对应关系，理解关键句子，即使词汇量有限的学生也可以通过关键词和逻辑推理做对这道题。

central_sentences.sentence: However, if the 'click' of an opening door is part of an ominous action such as a burglary, the sound mixer may call attention to the 'click' with an increase in volume; this helps to engage the audience in a moment of suspense.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，如果开门的"咔嗒"声是一个不祥动作的一部分，比如入室盗窃，音响师可能会通过增加音量来引起观众对"咔嗒"声的注意；这有助于让观众陷入紧张的时刻。

target_sen.target_sen: Synchronous sounds contribute to the realism of film and also help to create a particular atmosphere.
For example, the 'click' of a door being opened may simply serve to convince the audience that the image portrayed is real, and the audience may only subconsciously note the expected sound.

However, if the 'click' of an opening door is part of an ominous action such as a burglary, the sound mixer may call attention to the 'click' with an increase in volume; this helps to engage the audience in a moment of suspense.

groups.desc:
Questions 19-23

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Audiences are likely to be surprised if a film lacks background music.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“audiences”对应原文中的“we”，“surprised”对应“noticeable”，“lacks background music”对应“absence”。原文提到背景音乐已经变得无处不在，以至于它的缺席会被注意到，这暗示了观众会对没有背景音乐感到惊讶。因此，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.
central_sentences.translation: 我们可能都很熟悉电影中的背景音乐，它已经变得如此无处不在，以至于在缺席时会被注意到。

target_sen.target_sen: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.
Usually not meant to be noticeable, it often provides a tone or an emotional attitude toward the story and/or the characters depicted.
We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.

questions.title: Background music may anticipate a development in a film.
questions.analysis:
题干中的“Background music may anticipate a development in a film”可以通过以下同义替换词找到对应关系：
 - “Background music” 对应原文中的“background music”。
 - “anticipate” 对应原文中的“foreshadows”。
 - “development” 对应原文中的“change”。

 原文中的答案句“Background music often foreshadows a change in mood”明确指出背景音乐可以预示情绪的变化，这与题干中的“anticipate a development”意思相同。因此，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: background music often foreshadows a change in mood. For
central_sentences.translation: 背景音乐经常预示着情绪的变化。

target_sen.target_sen: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.
In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood.
In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood.

questions.title: Background music has more effect on some people than on others.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“背景音乐对某些人的影响比对其他人更大”，而原文中并没有提到背景音乐对不同人群的不同影响程度。原文只讨论了背景音乐的功能和作用，但没有涉及其对不同人群的不同影响。因此，答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence. We are aware that it is used to add emotion and rhythm.
central_sentences.translation: 我们可能都熟悉电影中的背景音乐，它已经变得如此普遍，以至于在缺席时才会被注意到。我们知道它被用来增添情感和节奏。

target_sen.target_sen: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.
In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood.
We are aware that it is used to add emotion and rhythm.
Usually not meant to be noticeable, it often provides a tone or an emotional attitude toward the story and/or the characters depicted.
We are aware that it is used to add emotion and rhythm.

questions.title: Background music may help the audience to make certain connections within the film.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为题干中的信息 "Background music may help the audience to make certain connections within the film" 在文章中有直接对应的表达 "Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes"。题干中的 "help" 对应文章中的 "aid"，"connections" 对应 "linking"，"within the film" 对应 "scenes"。这些同义替换词明确表明背景音乐可以帮助观众在电影中建立联系，因此答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes.
central_sentences.translation: 背景音乐可以通过连接场景来帮助观众理解。

target_sen.target_sen: Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes.
Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes.

questions.title: Audiences tend to be aware of how the background music is affecting them.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“audiences tend to be aware of how the background music is affecting them”意思是观众倾向于意识到背景音乐对他们的影响。而原文中提到“background music”通常“不应被注意到”（not meant to be noticeable），这与题干的意思正好相反。因此，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Usually not meant to be noticeable, it often provides a tone or an emotional attitude toward the story and/or the characters depicted.
central_sentences.translation: 背景音乐通常不应被注意到，它常常为故事和/或所描绘的角色提供一种基调或情感态度。

target_sen.target_sen: We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.
In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood.
We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.
Usually not meant to be noticeable, it often provides a tone or an emotional attitude toward the story and/or the characters depicted.

groups.desc:
Questions 24-26

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-E, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: when the audience listens to the dialogue.
if the film reflects the audience's own concerns.
if voice, sound and music are combined appropriately.
when the director is aware of how the audience will respond.
when the actor's appearance, voice and moves are consistent with each other.

questions.title: The audience's response to different parts of a film can be controlled
questions.analysis: 文章中提到，声音、音效和音乐的混合和平衡能够产生必要的强调，从而创造期望的效果。这与题干中观众反应被控制的描述完全一致。因此，正确答案是 C: if voice, sound and music are combined appropriately。

central_sentences.sentence: These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects.
central_sentences.translation: 这三个音轨必须混合和平衡，以产生必要的强调，从而创造出所需的效果。

target_sen.target_sen: The entire sound track consists of three essential ingredients: the human voice, sound effects and music.
These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects.
They include dialogue, synchronous and asynchronous sound effects, and music.
These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects.
These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects.

questions.title: The feelings and motivations of characters become clear
questions.analysis: 题干中的“feelings and motivations of characters”在原文中找到了对应的表达“expresses feelings and motivations of characters”。原文明确指出对话（dialogue）在表达角色的情感和动机方面的作用，因此正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: As is the case with stage drama, dialogue serves to tell the story and expresses feelings and motivations of characters as well.
central_sentences.translation: 就像舞台戏剧一样，对话不仅用于讲述故事，还用于表达角色的感受和动机。

target_sen.target_sen: As is the case with stage drama, dialogue serves to tell the story and expresses feelings and motivations of characters as well.
As is the case with stage drama, dialogue serves to tell the story and expresses feelings and motivations of characters as well.

questions.title: A character seems to be a real person rather than an actor
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是E，因为文章中提到“当声音质地与表演者的外貌和手势相匹配时，一个完整且非常逼真的角色就会出现”，这与题干中“角色看起来像真人而不是演员”的意思相符。选项E中的“appearance, voice and moves are consistent with each other”正是对文章中“physiognomy and gestures”和“voice textures”的同义替换。

central_sentences.sentence: When voice textures fit the performer's physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges.
central_sentences.translation: 当声音纹理与表演者的外貌和姿态相符时，一个完整且非常逼真的角色形象就会呈现出来。

target_sen.target_sen: When voice textures fit the performer's physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges.
When voice textures fit the performer's physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges.
When voice textures fit the performer's physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges.

content.title: 'This Marvellous Invention'

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place. Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human. Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it. Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: But language is foremost not just because it came first. In its own right it is a tool of extraordinary sophistication, yet based on an idea of ingenious simplicity: 'this marvellous invention of composing out of twenty-five or thirty sounds that infinite variety of expressions which, whilst having in themselves no likeness to what is in our mind, allow us to disclose to others its whole secret, and to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul'. This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement. Even so, there is just one flaw in all these hymns of praise, for the homage to language's unique accomplishment conceals a simple yet critical incongruity. Language is mankind's greatest invention – except, of course, that it was never invented. This apparent paradox is at the core of our fascination with language, and it holds many of its secrets.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Language often seems so skillfully drafted that one can hardly imagine it as anything other than the perfected handiwork of a master craftsman. How else could this instrument make so much out of barely three dozen measly morsels of sound? In themselves, these configurations of mouth – p, f, b, v, t, d, k, g, sh, a, e and so on – amount to nothing more than a few haphazard spits and splutters, random noises with no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain. But run them through the cogs and wheels of the language machine, let it arrange them in some very special orders, and there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do: from sighing the interminable boredom of existence to unravelling the fundamental order of the universe.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The most extraordinary thing about language, however, is that one doesn't have to be a genius to set its wheels in motion. The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion. Yet it is precisely this deceptive ease which makes language a victim of its own success, since in everyday life its triumphs are usually taken for granted. The wheels of language run so smoothly that one rarely bothers to stop and think about all the resourcefulness and expertise that must have gone into making it tick. Language conceals art.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of language's design. One of the showiest stunts that some languages can pull off is an ability to build up words of breath-breaking length, and thus express in one word what English takes a whole sentence to say. The Turkish word şehriliştiremediklerimizdensiniz, to take one example, means nothing less than 'you are one of those whom we can't turn into a town-dweller'. (In case you were wondering, this monstrosity really is one word, not merely many different words squashed together - most of its components cannot even stand up on their own.)

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history. A Sumerian word like munintuma'a ('when he had made it suitable for her') might seem rather trim compared to the Turkish colossus above. What is so impressive about it, however, is not its lengthiness but rather the reverse - the thrifty compactness of its construction. The word is made up of different slots, each corresponding to a particular portion of meaning. This sleek design allows single sounds to convey useful information, and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific. If you were to ask which bit in the Sumerian word corresponds to the pronoun 'it' in the English translation 'when he had made it suitable for her', then the answer would have to be nothing. Mind you, a very particular kind of nothing: the nothing that stands in the empty slot in the middle. The technology is so fine-tuned then that even a non-sound, when carefully placed in a particular position, has been invested with a specific function. Who could possibly have come up with such a nifty contraption?

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 27-32
The text has 6 sections.
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

list.text: Differences between languages highlight their impressiveness
The way in which a few sounds are organised to convey a huge range of meaning
Why the sounds used in different languages are not identical
Why language is the most important invention of all
The universal ability to use language

questions.analysis: Section A的内容主要强调了语言在所有人类发明中的重要性。通过对比其他发明，文章指出语言是使我们成为人类的关键，并且所有其他成就都依赖于语言。这与小标题vi: Why language is the most important invention of all完全契合。

central_sentences.sentence: Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place.
central_sentences.translation: 在人类的所有创造中，语言必须占据首要地位。

central_sentences.sentence: Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
central_sentences.translation: 与语言相比，所有其他发明都相形见绌，因为我们所有的成就都依赖于语言，并源于语言。

target_sen.target_sen: Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place.
Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place.
Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place.
Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.
Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.
Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place.
Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.
Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.

questions.analysis: 这段文字的核心在于语言的矛盾特性，即语言被认为是人类最伟大的发明，但实际上它从未被发明过。这种矛盾特性（incongruity和paradox）正是选项iv: Apparently incompatible characteristics of language所描述的内容。因此，选项iv是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Even so, there is just one flaw in all these hymns of praise, for the homage to language's unique accomplishment conceals a simple yet critical incongruity. Language is mankind's greatest invention – except, of course, that it was never invented. This apparent paradox is at the core of our fascination with language, and it holds many of its secrets.
central_sentences.translation: 即便如此，在所有这些赞美之词中还是存在一个缺陷，因为对语言独特成就的敬意掩盖了一个简单却关键的不一致之处。语言是人类最伟大的发明——但是，当然，它从未被发明过。这个明显的悖论是我们对语言着迷的核心，也蕴含了语言的许多秘密。

target_sen.target_sen: Even so, there is just one flaw in all these hymns of praise, for the homage to language's unique accomplishment conceals a simple yet critical incongruity.
This apparent paradox is at the core of our fascination with language, and it holds many of its secrets.

questions.analysis: Section C 主要讨论了语言如何通过少量的声音组合来表达丰富的意义。关键句子强调了语言如何通过少量的声音（"three dozen measly morsels of sound"）来表达大量的意义（"make so much" 和 "there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do"）。这与小标题ii: "The way in which a few sounds are organised to convey a huge range of meaning" 完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: How else could this instrument make so much out of barely three dozen measly morsels of sound? In themselves, these configurations of mouth – p, f, b, v, t, d, k, g, sh, a, e and so on – amount to nothing more than a few haphazard spits and splutters, random noises with no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain. But run them through the cogs and wheels of the language machine, let it arrange them in some very special orders, and there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do: from sighing the interminable boredom of existence to unravelling the fundamental order of the universe.
central_sentences.translation: 这种工具怎么能仅仅用三十多个微不足道的声音碎片就创造出如此丰富的内容呢？这些口腔构造——p、f、b、v、t、d、k、g、sh、a、e等等——本身不过是一些杂乱无章的吐音和咕哝，是没有意义、无法表达、无力解释的随机噪音。但是，当它们通过语言机器的齿轮和轮子，以某些特殊的顺序排列组合时，这些无意义的气流就无所不能：从叹息生存的无尽无聊到揭示宇宙的基本秩序。

syn_ary.synonym: three dozen measly morsels of sound

target_sen.target_sen: How else could this instrument make so much out of barely three dozen measly morsels of sound?

But run them through the cogs and wheels of the language machine, let it arrange them in some very special orders, and there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do: from sighing the interminable boredom of existence to unravelling the fundamental order of the universe.

In themselves, these configurations of mouth – p, f, b, v, t, d, k, g, sh, a, e and so on – amount to nothing more than a few haphazard spits and splutters, random noises with no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain.

questions.analysis: Section D的核心在于强调语言的普遍性和易用性，这与小标题vii: The universal ability to use language直接对应。通过识别段落中的关键词和使用排除法，学生可以更准确地选择正确的标题。

central_sentences.sentence: The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion.
central_sentences.translation: 语言机器让几乎每个人——从亚热带草原上的前现代采集者到郊区蔓延的后现代哲学家——都能将这些无意义的声音组合成无限多样的微妙意义，而且似乎不费吹灰之力。

target_sen.target_sen: The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion.

The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion.

questions.analysis: 这段话强调了不同语言的独特性和复杂性，正是这些差异突显了语言设计的奇妙之处。这与小标题i "Differences between languages highlight their impressiveness"（语言之间的差异突显了它们的令人印象深刻之处）直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of language's design.
central_sentences.translation: 通常，只有当我们遇到那些具有许多异国情调和奇特特征的外国语言时，才会让我们意识到语言设计的奇妙之处。

target_sen.target_sen: Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of language's design.
Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of language's design.
Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of language's design.
(In case you were wondering, this monstrosity really is one word, not merely many different words squashed together - most of its components cannot even stand up on their own.)

questions.analysis: Section F的主要内容是讨论语言中“无声”或“声音的缺失”也可以传达特定的意义。这与小标题v: Even silence can be meaningful直接对应。原文中的“absence of a sound”与“silence”同义，而“express something specific”与“meaningful”同义。因此，Section F的最佳标题是v。

central_sentences.sentence: What is so impressive about it, however, is not its lengthiness but rather the reverse - the thrifty compactness of its construction. The word is made up of different slots, each corresponding to a particular portion of meaning. This sleek design allows single sounds to convey useful information, and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，令人印象深刻的并非它的长度，而是恰恰相反——它结构的精简紧凑性。这个词由不同的位置组成，每个位置对应特定的一部分意义。这种精巧的设计允许单个声音传达有用的信息，事实上，甚至声音的缺失也被用来表达特定的内容。

target_sen.target_sen: This sleek design allows single sounds to convey useful information, and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific.
This sleek design allows single sounds to convey useful information, and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific.

groups.title: The importance of language
groups.desc:
Questions 33-36
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-G, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.
groups.collect:
The wheel is one invention that has had a major impact on $1255$aspects of life, but no impact has been as $1256$as that of language. Language is very $1257$, yet composed of just a small number of sounds.
Language appears to be $1258$to use. However, its sophistication is often overlooked.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“aspects of life”在文章中对应的表达是“material existence”。文章明确提到“the wheel... transformed our material existence”，这与题干中的“aspects of life”直接对应。因此，正确答案是“material”。

central_sentences.sentence: Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.
central_sentences.translation: 其他发明——如轮子、农业、切片面包——可能改变了我们的物质生活，但语言的出现才使我们成为人类。

target_sen.target_sen: Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.
Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 G: fundamental。文章中提到“Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance”，这表明语言的影响是最根本的。题干中的“no impact has been as 【 】as that of language”与此对应，因此“fundamental”是最合适的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
central_sentences.translation: 与语言相比，所有其他发明都相形见绌，因为我们所有的成就都依赖于语言并源于语言。

target_sen.target_sen: Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.
Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 B: complex。题干中的“very”对应原文中的“extraordinary”，“composed of just a small number of sounds”对应原文中的“based on an idea of ingenious simplicity”。这表明语言虽然复杂，但由少量的声音组成，因此答案是“complex”。

central_sentences.sentence: In its own right it is a tool of extraordinary sophistication, yet based on an idea of ingenious simplicity
central_sentences.translation: 语言本身是一种非常复杂的工具，但它基于一个非常简单的想法。

syn_ary.synonym: based on an idea of ingenious simplicity

target_sen.target_sen: In its own right it is a tool of extraordinary sophistication, yet based on an idea of ingenious simplicity: 'this marvellous invention of composing out of twenty-five or thirty sounds that infinite variety of expressions which, whilst having in themselves no likeness to what is in our mind, allow us to disclose to others its whole secret, and to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul'.

locating_words.locating_word: composed of just a small number of sounds

questions.analysis: 题干问的是“语言看起来是【 】使用的”，而文章中提到“显然不费吹灰之力（without the slightest exertion）”，这表明使用语言是很容易的。因此，正确答案是“easy”。

central_sentences.reason: display_answer=F(easy)，原文用doesn't have to be a genius/without exertion。
central_sentences.analysis: 不需天才且似乎不费力，体现“easy”。
central_sentences.keywords: doesn't have to be a genius, without the slightest exertion
sentences: The most extraordinary thing about language, however, is that one doesn't have to be a genius to set its wheels in motion.

The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: appears to be easy -> doesn't have to be a genius / without the slightest exertion; overlooked -> taken for granted

central_sentences.sentence: The most extraordinary thing about language, however, is that one doesn't have to be a genius to set its wheels in motion.

The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion.

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: Human beings might have achieved their present position without language.
questions.analysis: 题干问的是“人类是否可能在没有语言的情况下达到现在的地位”。原文明确指出“没有语言，我们不可能开始对其他动物甚至自然本身的无与伦比的掌控”，这直接否定了题干的假设。因此，答案是 NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.
central_sentences.translation: 没有语言，我们就不可能开始我们对所有其他动物，甚至对自然本身的无与伦比的支配力的攀升。

target_sen.target_sen: Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.
Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.

syn_ary.synonym: unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself

target_sen.target_sen: Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.

questions.title: The Port-Royal grammarians did justice to the nature of language.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Port-Royal grammarians did justice to the nature of language”意思是Port-Royal的语法学家们公正地揭示了语言的本质。文章中提到“the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement”，意思是Port-Royal的语法学家们提炼了语言的本质，并且没有人比他们更有力地赞美了其成就的伟大。这与题干的意思一致，因此答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port- Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement. Even
central_sentences.translation: 这就是1660年，凡尔赛附近的皇家港口修道院的著名法国语法学家们如何提炼出语言的本质，此后没有人能比他们更雄辩地赞颂语言成就的伟大。

syn_ary.synonym: the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles

target_sen.target_sen: This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port- Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement.

This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port- Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement.

This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port- Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement.

This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port- Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement.

questions.title: A complex idea can be explained more clearly in a sentence than in a single word.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息在原文中找不到明确的对应句子。虽然文章提到了语言的复杂性和表达能力，但并没有直接比较句子和单词在解释复杂想法上的清晰度。因此，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: one
central_sentences.translation: None

central_sentences.sentence: one
central_sentences.translation: None

questions.title: The Sumerians were responsible for starting the recording of events.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "The Sumerians were responsible for starting the recording of events." 可以通过以下同义替换在原文中找到对应关系：Sumerians 对应 the people，starting 对应 invented，recording of events 对应 documentation of history。因此，原文中的句子 "the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history." 直接支持了题干的陈述，说明苏美尔人确实负责开始了事件的记录。因此，答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history.
central_sentences.translation: 如果那听起来像是某种特例，那么请考虑苏美尔语，这种语言大约5000年前在幼发拉底河岸边被使用，使用它的人发明了书写，从而使历史记录成为可能。

target_sen.target_sen: And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history.

And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history.

And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history.
